news.title: Spain, Portugal step up scrutiny of soaring property markets
news.para: Spain and Portugal are closely watching their booming property markets, which show early signs of overheating with significant price hikes and strong mortgage lending. While regulators are introducing minor cooling measures, like adjusting debt-to-income ratios in Portugal, heavy intervention is unlikely. Current market conditions, though robust, are not mirroring the pre-2008 boom, with analysts suggesting supply constraints and a strong economy are driving current price growth.

news.title: Gillco Group to invest ₹800 crore to develop 12-acre housing project in Mohali
news.para: Gillco Group will invest about ₹700-800 crore to develop Gillco Meraqui, a 12-acre residential project in Sector 126, Mohali. The project will comprise 444 apartments, with total revenue potential estimated at ₹2,300-2,400 crore.

news.title: India office leasing hits 24.6 million sq ft in Q2 2026: Report
news.para: India’s office market recorded gross leasing of about 24.6 million sq ft in Q2 2026, the highest quarterly absorption on record, according to CBRE. New completions stood at about 21 million sq ft during the quarter, while GCCs accounted for 42% of total office space take-up.

news.title: Rentomojo gets Sebi's approval to float IPO
news.para: Rentomojo, the online rental and subscription platform for furniture and appliances, has secured Sebi's approval for its Initial Public Offering (IPO). The company plans to raise Rs 150 crore through a fresh issue and an Offer for Sale by existing shareholders. Proceeds will fund loan repayments, warehouse leases, and general corporate needs.

news.title: Prestige Estates to acquire 50% stake in Advent Convention and Hotels for ₹504 crore
news.para: Prestige Estates Projects will acquire a 50% stake in Advent Convention and Hotels International. The company plans to develop a commercial project in Andheri East, Mumbai, with a leasable area of about 1.50 million sq ft and GDV of about ₹4,500 crore.

news.title: Five years later, Greater Noida smart villages still battle broken roads
news.para: Five years after Greater Noida West's 14 villages were designated "smart villages" with promises of modern infrastructure, residents face neglected roads riddled with potholes and waterlogged craters. Despite a Rs 150 crore project launched in 2021, basic amenities like paved roads, streetlights, and proper drainage remain absent, leaving villagers frustrated and calling for tangible progress beyond mere announcements.

news.title: Brihanmumbai civic body asks builders to suspend hazardous constructions
news.para: Mumbai's civic body has ordered a halt to hazardous construction work and mandated stringent safety protocols for all project sites. This directive follows a 'red' alert for severe weather, emphasizing the critical need to secure scaffolding, cranes, and temporary structures. Builders must conduct immediate inspections, ensure worker safety with proper gear, and suspend high-risk operations until conditions improve, prioritizing public and worker well-being.

news.title: Nashik civic body finally scraps proposed greenfield township project at Makhmalabad
news.para: Nashik Municipal Corporation has officially cancelled the ambitious 753-acre Greenfield township project at Makhmalabad. The decision, confirmed by civic chief Manisha Khatri, follows strong opposition from farmers concerned about land rights and livelihoods, alongside legal challenges. The proposed Smart City Mission project aimed to accommodate future urban expansion but faced significant hurdles, ultimately leading to its termination after years of debate and court proceedings.

news.title: Telangana unveils draft CURE bill; aims to replace 1955 GHMC Act
news.para: Telangana's government has introduced the draft CURE Bill, 2026, to replace the outdated GHMC Act, 1955. This new legislation aims to modernize urban governance for a rapidly expanding Hyderabad, introducing reforms in environmental sustainability, public safety, and inclusive development. Key proposals include a Climate Action Framework, Gender Inclusion Cell, and a dedicated Disaster Management Authority, seeking public feedback by July 24.

news.title: Telangana: Forensic audit finds 22,000 anomalies in Dharani transactions
news.para: A forensic audit of eight lakh land transactions in Telangana has uncovered nearly 22,000 anomalies, involving around 10,000 acres.  The state government is struggling to identify those responsible due to tampered records and deleted data, with suspicious transfers of assigned lands reported in several districts.  The Dharani portal's vulnerabilities were exploited, prompting a directive for a new, secure system and a committee to investigate.

news.title: Six more chief minister's co-working spaces to open soon in Chennai
news.para: Six new chief minister's co-working spaces, established by the previous DMK government in North Chennai, are set to open to the public. The Greater Chennai Corporation is outsourcing housekeeping services for these facilities, which offer affordable workstations and study areas for competitive exam aspirants. These centers, including locations in Purasaiwalkam and Kolathur, aim to provide accessible learning environments, with plans for further expansion and contractor-based maintenance in the future.

news.title: Indore civic body cancels licences of five consultants over permit fraud
news.para: Indore Municipal Corporation has cracked down on building permit fraud, revoking licenses of five consultants and engineers.  An investigation revealed widespread manipulation of the automated 'deemed approval' system, where applicants evaded fees and under-reported construction areas.  IMC is now reviewing all digital permits to recover dues and enforce regulations, barring the penalized professionals from practice.

news.title: Verify clients against latest UN sanctions list: Odisha RERA to real estate agents
news.para: Odisha real estate agents are now mandated to implement anti-money laundering protocols, verifying clients against UN sanctions lists. This directive, following international updates on terror financing watchlists, requires enhanced due diligence for any client matching restricted profiles. Failure to comply could lead to penal action, ensuring stricter adherence to global security measures in property transactions.

news.title: Chandigarh wants to rise higher but can its roads take the load?
news.para: Chandigarh's ambitious development plans, including higher building limits and mixed land use, are raising serious concerns about traffic congestion.  Official reports and resident objections highlight a critical gap: the city is densifying without a clear plan for its overloaded transport infrastructure.  Decades-old warnings about saturated roads and a lack of mass transit are now a stark reality, with private vehicles dominating and speeds plummeting.

news.title: Ludhiana: LIT slashes reserve price to ₹182 crore in fresh bid to auction its commercial building
news.para: Ludhiana Improvement Trust has slashed the reserve price of a prime commercial building by ₹15 crore to ₹182 crore in a fresh e-auction, hoping to attract buyers after previous attempts failed. The 18-year-old, abandoned property, featuring extensive commercial space and parking, has suffered from neglect. LIT is also considering price cuts for other properties due to poor market response.

news.title: NCLT directs Entertainment City to register minority stake in favour of Parmesh Construction
news.para: Insolvency tribunal NCLT has greenlit Parmesh Construction Company's acquisition of a 4.26% stake in Entertainment City, dismissing Unitech Holding's objections. The tribunal ruled that the share transfer by minority investors was valid, despite Unitech's claims of violated shareholder agreements. This decision stems from a broader 100% disinvestment process for Entertainment City, where Parmesh Construction emerged as the successful bidder.

news.title: Manesar civic body razes six concrete plants, 30 more under scanner
news.para: Authorities in Gurugram have intensified their crackdown on illegal operations, demolishing six ready-mix concrete plants on Garhi-Basai Road for lacking mandatory permissions. This action follows a similar demolition last week. Despite these efforts, over 30 unauthorized plants remain operational, raising concerns about regulatory oversight. Officials vow to take action against all remaining illegal establishments, urging businesses to secure necessary approvals before commencing operations.

news.title: Varanasi: VDA intensifies infrastructure development at three proposed townships
news.para: Varanasi Development Authority is set to launch plot registrations for its flagship Anand Kashi Yojana by August end, marking a significant township project after 32 years. The 250-acre development, inspired by Kashi's heritage, will feature extensive infrastructure, including roads, green spaces, and civic amenities.  This project, along with Rudra Vihar and Sports City, is receiving substantial government backing and is closely monitored by the Chief Minister.

news.title: Navi Mumbai civic body nets ₹434 crore in property tax in Q1 FY27
news.para: Navi Mumbai Municipal Corporation has achieved a record Rs 434.02 crore in property tax collection for the first quarter of 2026-27, a significant rise from the previous year. This success is attributed to technology-driven administration, data analytics, and enhanced digital payment options, with over 72% of taxes paid online. The initiative also recovered dues from chronic defaulters, showcasing citizen trust and contribution to city development.

news.title: Haryana caps daily property registration appointments at 20 per office to ensure faster services
news.para: Haryana has introduced a new token system for property registrations, limiting daily appointments to 20 per tehsil and sub-divisional office. This random allocation aims to prevent favoritism and speed up the process for citizens. The move is expected to enhance transparency, reduce waiting times, and curb the influence of middlemen, making property dealings more efficient and citizen-friendly across the state.
